latest.title: Update to Admission of Incentive Shares
latest.content:
Today 15:23
RNS Number : 1810V
Metals Exploration PLC
03 March 2026
METALS EXPLORATION PLC
Update to Admission of Incentive Shares and Total Voting Rights
Metals Exploration plc (AIM: MTL) ("Metals Exploration" or the "Company"), a gold producer, exploration, and development company with assets in the Philippines and Nicaragua, announces that, further to the Company's announcement of 27 February 2026, the admission of 1,182,210 new Ordinary Shares in accordance with the 2025 MIP will now take place at 8.00 a.m. on or around 6 March 2026 ("Admission").
Following Admission, the Company will have 3,279,456,030 Ordinary Shares in issue, of which 299,385,458 Ordinary Shares are held in treasury and, accordingly, the total number of voting rights is 2,980,070,572. Therefore, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculation by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure and Transparency Rules.
For further information, please contact or visit:

Metals Exploration PLC (AIM: MTL) ("Metals Exploration" or the "Company"), a gold producer, development and exploration company with assets in the Philippines and Nicaragua, announces that it has received notice of exercise of an option in respect of 1,218,396 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each ("Ordinary Shares") with an exercise price of £0.0829 each raising a total of £101,005.

latest.title: Exercise of Warrants and Options
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6040U
Metals Exploration PLC
27 February 2026
METALS EXPLORATION PLC
Exercise of Warrants and Options, Issue of Incentive Shares and Total Voting Rights
Metals Exploration plc (AIM: MTL) ("Metals Exploration" or the "Company"), a gold producer, exploration, and development company with assets in the Philippines and Nicaragua, announces that it has received notices of exercise in respect of, in aggregate, 3,334,556 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each ("Ordinary Shares") in relation to (i) 2,584,556 warrants with an exercise price of £0.0605 each, raising £156,365.64; and (ii) the exercise of an option for 750,000 Ordinary Shares with an option price of £0.0829 each, raising £62,175.
Issue of Incentive Shares under the Management Incentive Programme ("MIP")
Additionally, the Company has issued a total of 1,182,210 new Ordinary Shares at an issue price of £0.1543 to members of the senior management team, in accordance with the 2025 MIP.
Details of the awards to PDMRs are set out below, with further information being contained in the Appendix to this announcement:

Following Admission, the Company will have 3,278,237,634 Ordinary Shares in issue, of which 299,385,458 ordinary shares are held in treasury, and, accordingly, the total number of voting rights is 2,978,852,176. Therefore, this figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculation by which they may determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Exercise of Warrants, Grant of Options, and TVR
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4812S
Metals Exploration PLC
11 February 2026
METALS EXPLORATION PLC
Exercise of Warrants, Grant of Options, and Total Voting Rights
Metals Exploration PLC (AIM: MTL) ("Metals Exploration" or the "Company"), a gold producer, development and exploration company with assets in the Philippines and Nicaragua, announces that (1) it has received a notice of warrant exercise in respect of 150,000 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each ("Ordinary Shares") with an exercise price of £0.0605 each; and (2) the Company has granted options over 12,000,000 Ordinary Shares to two senior executives, in line with its existing Long-Term Incentive Plan ("LTIP") scheme, as detailed below.
Long-Term Incentive Plan Options ("LTIP Options")
At the General Meeting of shareholders held on 23 August 2024, a LTIP was approved, granting the Directors authority to issue up to 15% of the Company's issued capital as LTIP Options. In accordance with this approval the Company has issued a further 12 million options.
The key terms of these LTIP Options are as follows:
·
Exercise price: £0.0001 per Ordinary Share.
·
Exercise period: 7 years from issue.
Vesting conditions
:
·
A minimum consecutive continuing employment of three years with or, as applicable, a service relationship as a non-executive director, consultant, contractor or other adviser to the Company following each performance condition summarised below having been met. The relevant dates are 31 March 2028 regards to the 7,000,000 LTIP Options and 15 January 2029 regards to 5,000,000 LTIP Options; and
·
One-third of the options shall vest upon the earliest date following the grant date upon which the value of an Ordinary Share (based on the volume-weighted average price over a 30-day period) is at least:
7,000,000
(i) 10 pence; (ii) 15 pence; and (iii) 20 pence.
5,000,000
(i) 20 pence; (ii) 25 pence; and (iii) 30 pence.
Admission and Total Voting Rights
Application has been made to the London Stock Exchange PLC for the admission of 150,000 new Ordinary Shares to trading on AIM ("Admission"), which is expected to take place at 8.00 a.m. on or around 12 February 2026.
Following Admission, the Company will have 3,259,247,368 Ordinary Shares in issue, of which 299,385,458 Ordinary Shares are held in treasury, and, accordingly, the total number of voting rights is 2,959,861,910. Therefore, this figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculation by which they may determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
For further information, please contact or visit:

company_30s.what_they_do: MTL Group plc is a small mining‑exploration company that owns a portfolio of mineral assets, mainly in metals such as copper, zinc and rare‑earths. It focuses on early‑stage exploration and development projects across Europe and adjacent regions.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The metals it targets are essential to modern technology and renewable‑energy supply chains. A successful project would feed the growing demand for copper in electric vehicles, solar panels and data‑centre infrastructure.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the exploration phase with no production yet. Recent corporate filings show a focus on managing its share structure and maintaining regulatory compliance rather than delivering operational milestones. Trading volume is thin and the stock sits near the bottom of its historical range.

rns_narrative: The latest RNS releases are largely governance‑related: updates to incentive‑share admission, option exercises, and regulatory rule confirmations. These are routine corporate actions that keep the share register tidy but do not signal a new project or cash‑injection.
An investor presentation and a video update were issued, but the content mainly reiterated existing plans and financial statements without new drilling or partnership news.
Overall, the company’s recent disclosures suggest it is maintaining liquidity and compliance, not pushing any new development agenda.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: The community is largely silent, with a few neutral or mildly bullish comments but no sustained chatter.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders hint that a positive drilling report or a new joint‑venture could spark a rebound, but the tone remains speculative.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most skeptics point to the lack of tangible progress, the thin trading base and the absence of a clear catalyst as reasons to stay away.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed flat; no recent surge in activity or sentiment shift has been observed.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker are effectively dead, indicating no public search interest.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of a new exploration lease, drilling results or a partnership with a larger miner.
A capital‑raising event or equity issuance that improves liquidity and signals confidence in the company’s prospects.
A regulatory filing that confirms a new project or a change in the company’s asset portfolio.
A scheduled quarterly update that includes any positive operational data or a revised outlook.
Any unexpected media coverage or analyst endorsement that brings the stock into broader attention.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor for a sudden spike in volume, a clear price move above a key support level, or a press release that mentions a new project or drilling results. Pay particular attention to the next RNS cycle and any change in the share‑structure filings.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the very early stages of a potential recovery; there is no clear evidence of a deep crash yet. If an APEX score were high, it would indicate tight compression ready for a breakout, but the current data do not confirm that.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the absence of a clear crash signal, coupled with a lack of catalysts, makes a decisive bullish stance premature.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Should the company release a positive drilling report or secure a partnership, the low base price would allow a substantial upside. A rebound to the upper half of the 52‑week range would represent a significant gain over the current trough.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without operational news, the stock may remain stagnant or fall further. The thin trading base could amplify volatility and liquidity risk, limiting any potential upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is high if a catalyst materialises, but the downside is effectively the current low. The risk‑reward profile is asymmetric, favouring large upside if a trigger occurs, but the probability of such a trigger is uncertain.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to a well‑tested support level, size the position at about 10 % of capital, and set a stop just below that support to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike or a positive RNS about a new project. Target a price near the mid‑52‑week range as a first resistance level.
playbook.if_passing: If the company faces delisting, bankruptcy, or a definitive negative announcement (e.g., a failed drilling campaign), exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With no clear crash or catalyst yet, the setup remains uncertain; a breakthrough announcement could unlock upside from the current low, but the risk of stagnation or further decline is real.
